Among the Indian population, about 28% in the rural and 26% in the urban areas are estimated to be 2below the poverty line , which is defined as the expenditure needed to obtain, on an average, 2400 Kcal per capita per day in the rural areas and 2100 Kcal in urban areas. Long-term malnutrition leads to stunting and wasting, non-communicable chronic diet related disorders, increased morbidity and mortality and reduced physical work output. It is a great economic loss to the country and undermines development. Protein Energy Malnutrition (PEM), micronutrient deficiencies such as vitamin A deficiency (VAD) , Iron
